verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B (ATP5B) is canonically the beta catalytic subunit of mitochondrial Complex V (inner mitochondrial membrane). However, robust multi-study evidence documents ectopic surface display (eATP synthase) on the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane in cancer cells (NSCLC, leukemia, prostate cancer, hepatocellular carcinoma) and vascular endothelial cells. The intact ATP synthase complex is assembled in mitochondria and trafficked to the PM via DRP1/KIF5B-mediated mitochondrial membrane-PM fusion. Flow cytometry, surface biotinylation, and IHC on intact tumor cells confirm outer-leaflet accessibility of ATP5B. Anti-ATP5B antibodies targeting the cell surface have been developed as therapeutic candidates. Surface display is cell-state/oncogenic-transformation-dependent, qualifying as cell_state_induced contextual.
